name: Drew Allar
scouting_report: Owns excellent size with prototypical build of a big-bodied pro-style quarterback. Nearly 6-foot-5 and 230-plus pounds and wears it well. Arm strength reflects those physical tools. Ball gets out fast despite long-levered build. Displays big-armed vertical juice with terrific velocity in short-to-intermediate situations. Knows how and when to vary zip and does so from myriad arm angles with impressive playmaking creativity. Shows ability to suddenly and smoothly dial back power, if needed, at tail end of delivery. Not a true dual-threat, but capable of scrambling when the play breaks down. Throw power transfers to off-schedule, off-platform opportunities. Strong enough to make timely, accurate cross-body throws on the move. Functional athleticism derives in part from a foundation at other positions earlier in career. Shows anticipatory feel for the position. Strong production as a junior improved to outstanding during senior season. Only seven interceptions in 514 throws (one in 73.4 attempts) in 2021. May need time to adjust to speed of the game and processing rate at the next level. Most naturally talented thrower in the 2022 quarterback class. Not tapped out by any means and possesses immense upside. Should become an impact high-major starter with NFL Draft first-round potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2021: Led Medina to a 13-1 record and an Ohio Division I Region I runner-up finish.2020: Led Medina to an Ohio Division I Region I runner-up finish.

name: Cade Klubnik
scouting_report: Good height with a lean, athletic, somewhat narrow build. Mechanics have noticeably improved with reps and experience. A natural at the position. Competitive but calm and poised. One of the most accurate passers in the class whose ball placement and touch stand out. Good pocket awareness with functional athleticism to extend plays when necessary. Good athlete when he gets loose in the open field. Increasingly impressive improvisational ability as an off-schedule playmaker. Varies velocities and touch deftly. Can make all the throws, whether over-the-middle, intermediate bullets, or field-stretching deep balls. Outstanding production vs. Texas Class 6A competition for state championship-caliber team. Impresses in live game action and elite camp settings.  Competed in track and field (400 meters, triple jump, long jump) earlier in high school. Occasionally will take unnecessary chances, but owns an exceptional INT rate over a full two seasons starting. Undoubtedly one of the top 2022 quarterbacks in the country. Projects as a multi-year impact starter at the high-major level with NFL Draft high-round potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso has competed in track and field.Ran 51.52 in the 400 as a sophomore, while long jumping 19-10 and triple jumping 41-8.2020: Led Austin Westlake -- ranked No. 3 in the country by MaxPreps -- to a 14-0 record and the Texas 6A D-I state title as a junior; Texas District 26-6A Offensive MVP; MaxPreps first-team Junior All-American; Was named the TSWA Class 6A Offensive Player of the Year2019: Served as a reserve on Austin Westlake's 15-1 Texas 6A D-II state championship team as a sophomore.

name: Conner Weigman
scouting_report: Adept pocket passer that displays rhythmic timing and great touch on passes outside the numbers. Weigman has the intellectual capacity to handle checks at the line of scrimmage and in-play processing skills in a pro-style offense. Weigman does not have first round physical traits but has plenty of arm to make requisite throws. Weigman projects as a late round NFL draft pick with the upside to be a day two pick if he stays healthy, as he's been injured in 2023 and 2024.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMulti-sport athlete who is also one of the top baseball prospects in Texas2021: Texas District 16-6A Offensive MVP as a senior; Named the Houston Touchdown Club Offensive Player of the Year; Named the National High School Quarterback of the Year by the National Quarterback Club2020: Was named the District 16-6A Offensive Player of the Year as a junior2019: Texas District 14-6A Offensive Newcomer of the Year as a sophomore.

name: Ty Simpson
scouting_report: Possesses adequate size for his position at 6-foot-1 plus and a shade under 200-pounds. Has growth potential to add additional weight to his frame at the next level. Displays good athletic ability in the pocket and above average mobility as a runner. Predominant pocket passer, comes from a spread run-pass option scheme in high school. Demonstrates good foot quickness and body quickness in the pocket, showing a natural ability to extend plays with his feet in or outside the pocket. Plays with good feel and instincts as a passer, keeping his eyes down field while withstanding pressure. Appears to have a clean and abbreviated throwing motion, demonstrating the ability to get the ball out quickly and from a variety of angles. Displays good arm strength as a passer, showing the ability to make every throw on the field. Flashes accuracy at every level possessing an ability to throw his receivers open and play with anticipation. Has a poise to his passing game in the pocket but also excels with his ability to improvise and extend plays with his feet. More of a passer, but you have to respect his feet as a runner. Rushed for over 1200 yards and 15 TD’s on the ground in his senior season. Balanced in both his abilities as a passer and runner, Simpson poses a multi-dimensional threat for opposing defenses at the next level, showing the ability to play within or outside the structure of an offensive attack. Projects to an all-conference, potential all-american prospect at the next level. Needs one season to sit and develop as he acclimates to the speed of the game at the collegiate level, but possesses the ability to be a multi-year high level performer for a Power Five program.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Led Westview to a second-round playoff appearance in Tennessee Division I Class 3A as a junior.

name: Devin Brown
scouting_report: Long, wiry frame with room to add muscle mass. Carries 190 lbs. well and continues to fill out. Dynamic quarterback with basketball background. Shows very good arm strength and zip on his passes. Flashes ability to make off-platform throws without losing velocity. Distributes to all levels of the field and looks extremely comfortable rolling out to space. Displays good timing and anticipation over the middle. Mechanically sound and quick to release the football. Athletic and tough enough to plant his foot and gain yards with his legs. Could continue to improve decision-making and tendency to chase the big play. Needs to improve his touch and loft in tighter windows. Potential multi-year starter at elite Power Five level and second-day NFL Draft projection.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPlayed at Queen Creek (Ariz.) before transferring to Draper (Utah) Corner Canyon for his senior season.2021: As a senior, led Corner Canyon to a 12-2 record and a Utah 6A state runner-up finish.2020: Led Queen Creek to a 6-3 record as a junior and an Arizona 6A first-round playoff appearance.2019: Varsity as a sophomore and helped Queen Creek to a 9-4 record and the Arizona 6A state semifinals.

name: Walker Howard
scouting_report: Adequate frame with lean build that should add some mass in college. Established a following as an elite camp performer early in high school career before taking over varsity starting duties. Has posted encouraging testing numbers early in career (4.76 40, 4.40 shuttle). Consistent, accurate passer to all levels. Steps into throws and gets great zip on balls to the short-to-intermediate range. Posted stellar production in breakout junior campaign. Threw a lot of reps while also valuing the ball, as evidenced by only one INT per 63 pass attempts (five in 316 throws). Not a true dual-threat but functionally mobile with ability to extend plays outside the pocket. Remains accurate on the move. Squares shoulders and fires even when on the run. Plenty of arm strength but still probably some juice to squeeze out at the top end, which should come with add physical mass/strength. Typically stays on top of the ball but will have one sail a bit every once in a while. Possesses a good QB pedigree. Seems quite polished relative to age and could be a particularly high-floor QB at the next level. Projects to high-major competition with long-term NFL Draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Helped St. Thomas More to an 10-0 record and Louisiana Division I state championship in his first year as the starting quarterback.2019: Helped St. Thomas More to an 11-2 record and Louisiana Division I state championship as a sophomore.

name: Gunner Stockton
scouting_report: Thick built player with average height for the position.  He is a better athlete than on would think, He is able to make plays in the pocket when the rush breaks down by using his feel and presence for where the rush is coming from and how he needs to buy time. Does a good job of keeping his eyes downfield to make a play when he is flushed and on the move.  Shows good arm strength with the ability to make throws with touch when need be allowing for his receivers to make plays on the move to get extra yardage.  He has a quick release and is able to process if his 1st read isn't there to get through his progression. By throwing and running for a combined 4,709 yards passing and rushing along with 71 touchdowns as a junior you can see the growth in his game over that last couple of years.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2020: MaxPreps Junior All-American second-team selection; led Rabun County to a 12-2 record and a Georgia AA state semifinal appearance.2019: MaxPreps All-American first-team selection as a sophomore; led Rabun County to a 12-1 record and a Georgia third-round playoff appearance.2018: MaxPreps Freshman All-American selection; led Rabun County to an 11-2 record and a Georgia AA third-round playoff appearance.

name: Nick Evers
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Texas District 6-6A first-team QB selection as a junior.

name: Maalik Murphy
scouting_report: Physically gifted signal caller with a lot of tools. Still needs game reps and experience to take his game to another level. Has a clean, easy delivery and one of the strongest arms in the class. Has tightened his release over the last year, less of a windup and gets it out quick. Shows good feet in the pocket but can continue to improve extending plays and getting out of the pocket to make a throw or running for plus yards. Has a big frame but has maintained his weight and isn't sloppy. Shows good touch on the deep ball and looks good when throwing on time and in rhythm. A natural leader who teammates will love in the locker room. Mature and works hard off the field. Very intriguing at the next level and has a ton of upside and if he maximizes his potential, has a chance to be special.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMaalik Murphy is a Quarterback from Gardena, CA.

name: Brady Allen
scouting_report: A big high school quarterback who will have prototypical size as a college pocket passer. Varsity starter since early in his career and has a lot of game experience. Has the arm to make all the throws and has shown he can throw through wind and tough conditions without losing much velocity. Throws with good touch down the field and can drop the ball in over the top of coverage. Can also throw on the move without losing velocity. Elevated team throughout the course of his career and played well against good competition. Can throw on the run but is not as much of a running threat and has to show he can escape the rush at the Power Five level. Heavy shotgun in high school and may have to adjust to being under center more depending on college system. But a big prototype with a great arm that makes him a high end prospect and potential Sunday guy.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundBrady Allen is a 6-5, 196-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Fort Branch, IN.

name: Tayven Jackson
scouting_report: Athletic passer with good physical traits. Has height and the body to fill in. Multi-sport guy who does athletic tasks fluidly and easily on the field. Mohile and elusive in the pocket. Can take off and make plays with his feet. Gets rid of the ball quickly, does not try to hold it too long or sandlot play despite his athleticism. Did not throw a high volume of balls in high school compared to other top quarterback recruits and must continue to polish pure passing skills and mechanics. May need a year or two in college, but has a high ceiling.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundTayven Jackson is a Quarterback from Greenwood, IN.

name: AJ Duffy
scouting_report: A gamer of sorts that has flashed in a variety of different settings. Has a knack for making something out of nothing. Creative when the pocket breaks down and does a nice job of keeping his eyes down the field while on the move. Equipped with a live arm that has proven to be rather accurate when he’s got his A game going. Not afraid to take a deep shot or challenge a tight coverage window. Started prep career off in California where he lost his entire junior year due to global pandemic. Ended up transferring into the national powerhouse that is IMG Academy for his senior season where he directed a run-heavy offense. Still managed to complete just under 64 percent of his passes with 13 touchdowns to go against four interceptions. Must cut down on mistakes and limit errant throws when he gets to college, but has a chance to win games at the Power 5 level one day given his skillset as he’s more than capable of making high-level throws. Likely to find the most success in a balanced single-back spread attack that has plenty of RPOs on the call sheet.
athletic_background: H.S. name: Holden Geriner
scouting_report: Intriguing quarterback prospect given quick release, sound decision making process and ability to push the ball down the field. Capped off prep career by wining a state title as a senior in Georgia’s AAAA classification. Stands tall in the pocket and does a nice job of feeling out pressure. Has some experience with five and seven-step drops, but seems to be at his best when he’s making one or two reads and then firing to a spot or target. Isn’t afraid to challenge tight windows. More of a drive ball thrower, but does show some touch on his deeper passes. Shouldn’t be considered a real dual-threat talent, but can extend plays once the pocket breaks down and pick up yardage with his legs in certain situations. Completed just over 67 percent of his passes his final two years of high school tossing 61 touchdowns to go against just six interceptions. Likely to excel in more of a balanced spread attack that wants its quarterback to distribute the football, and quickly. Will likely need some time to adjust to the speed of the game on Saturdays, but has the skillset to eventually emerge as someone that can win at the Power 5 level. Could eventually suit up on Sundays if he keeps progressing.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2021: Atlanta Journal-Constitution AAAA Offensive Player of the Year. Led Benedictine to a 13-2 record and a state title as he completed just under 68 percent of his passes for 3,377 yards with 36 touchdowns to go against just three interceptions. Threw for a season-high 448 yards against Ware County. Tossed four touchdowns against Carver.

name: Sam Horn
scouting_report: Excellent height for the position with a moldable frame. Baseball background. A true gunslinger equipped with a live arm that’s always looking to make a play. Simple delivery motion, releasing over-the-top. Puts plenty of pace on the ball, but also understands how to use touch. Can make a variety of different throws. Mobile athlete with the foot quickness to escape the rush. Relatively accurate on the move. Will make people miss in the open field. Needs to take better care of the football and cut down on the interceptions. Must also improve timing and get better at working through his progressions. Highly productive at the prep level playing in a pass-happy system. Helped carry his team to the state finals as a junior playing in one of the Peach State’s highest classifications. Has the looks of someone that will develop into a starter at a Power 5 program. Tools and athletic profile suggests that he has a chance to play on Sundays.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball. Drawing interest from SEC schools. Older brother, Monty, is a pitcher at Kennesaw State.2020: Led Collins Hill to a 12-3 record and the AAAAAAA state final. Passed for over 3,900 yards and 41 touchdowns in a wide-open, pass-happy offense. Doubled as his team's punter.

name: Jacurri Brown
scouting_report: Impressive looking athlete with the desired height to play the position. Owns a leaner build now, but could eventually get up to 220 pounds or more. One of the more unique quarterback prospects in the class of 2022 given ability to run the football. Has already eclipsed the 100-yard mark rushing in 14 different varsity games. Still developing as a passer, but started to show some improvement the spring after junior season. Has no issues getting the ball to all different levels of the field. Likes to use trajectory to his advantage, especially on his deep shots. More of a drive-ball thrower, but capable of making off-plane throws on the run. Streaky player that’s not afraid to challenge tight windows, but struggles at times with accuracy and tends to make some of the easiest throws the most difficult. Tested exceptionally well before his senior season clocking a 4.6 in the 40-yard dash and a 4.4 in the short shuttle. Long stride allows him to pull away from defenses. Not afraid, however, to lower his shoulder and power through defenders, if needed. Considered a leader by those in his huddle and has won plenty of big games on Friday nights playing in one of the Peach State’s highest classifications. Will need to find some touch and keep improving his mechanics, but raw tools are valuable at the quarterback position, and he has a ton of them. Should be viewed as a high-ceiling, low-floor prospect that can make a difference at the Power 5 level with patience and the right coaching. Likely to thrive in an offensive system that embraces dual-threat quarterbacks and leans on his strengths.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Georgia AAAAAAA all-state first-team selection. Helped carry Lowndes to a 10-2 record and the state semifinals. Named Winnersville Classic MVP after totaling 276 yards of offense and four touchdowns.2019: Georgia Sports Writers Association AAAAAAA all-state first-team selection as an athlete. Led the Vikings to a state championship game appearance compiling a 14-1 record as a sophomore.2018: Started games on varsity as a freshman.

name: Justyn Martin
scouting_report: Long 6-4 quarterback with a ton of long term upside if he reaches his potential. A gifted thrower with a big arm and easy velocity. The ball jumps out of his hand and he can make every throw on the field with ease. He needs to continue to improve in how he quickly he processes the game and in his decision making but that should  come with more game reps. Still a relatively late bloomer and his development has been hurt a bit by lack of game experience due to Covid shortened junior season. He has worked hard to tighten his release over the last year and his motion is more compact and quicker. He’s a good athlete, a former basketball player and is able to extend plays and take off and run for big yards. Throws a tight ball and shows good touch on the deep ball as well. When he’s decisive in his reads, he’s very good and as the game continues to slow down for him, he has a chance to be a special player at the next level.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundLimited track experience as a sophomore in Spring 2020.Spring 2021: Played an abbreviated junior season. Led Inglewood to a 4-0 record as the Sentinels outscored opponents 206-62.

name: Zach Pyron
scouting_report: Possesses a thick frame at 6-foot-2 and 215-pounds. Displays above average athleticism and avoid-ability in the pocket. Flashes adequate mobility as a runner. Shows the ability to get what’s there. Demonstrates a clean shoulder high motion. Appears to have adequate to above average arm strength. Shows the ability to make a variety of throws; displaying the ability to throw with touch down the field. Excels with a moving pocket. Can get the ball out from a variety of arm angles. Will need to improve accuracy and ball placement as he continues to develop. Has some “gamer” qualities to him. Value his instincts within the pocket and ability to make plays on the run.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Led Pinson Valley to the Alabama 6A state title as a junior.2019: Starting QB as a sophomore, leading Fyffe to a second consecutive Alabama 2A state championship.

name: Jayden Denegal
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundJayden Denegal is a Quarterback from Apple Valley, CA.

name: Garret Rangel
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Texas District 5-5A D-I Offensive MVP as a junior; led Frisco Lone Star to a 7-4 record and a Texas 5A D-I third-round playoff appearance.2019: Texas District 5-5A D-I Offensive Newcomer of the Year as a sophomore; led Frisco Lone Star to a 14-1 record and Texas 5A D-I state semifinal appearance.

name: Nate Johnson
scouting_report: Speed, speed and more speed.  With an arm to boot.  Johnson is one of the fastest football players in the state,  not just quarterbacks, players, period, running a 10.56 100m this spring.  But lest you think he's just a running quarterback, he's more a quarterback that can run- Johnson still wants to make plays with his arm. He's got a unique skill set, with arm strength and a quicker, consistent release and a smooth motion.  He is boiling with upside and has all the natural physical tools to be a great quarterback.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMember of 247Sports’ 2022 Freaks List.

name: AJ Swann
scouting_report: A touch passer with a quicker release and above average pocket presence. Has shown the ability to not only push the ball down the field, but also challenge defenders and tight windows at various points throughout his prep career. Measured 6-foot-2 the summer before senior season. Owns a thicker build as he’s hovering 200 pounds and uses that weight to advantage as he isn’t afraid to take a hit. Also has a knack for moving the chains with his feet the unconventional way when a play breaks down. Clean more times than not with his drops and likes to step into his throws in hopes of generating more velocity. Completed just over 61 percent of his passes as a senior while playing in more of a pro-style spread offense. Must cutdown on mistakes at the next level after tossing over 20 interceptions the past three years, but has the skillset to win games at the Power 5 level. Could find success in a number of different offensive systems, but likely to be the most comfortable in the gun directing a balanced attack.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2021: Helped Cherokee make opening round of Peach State's AAAAAAA playoffs. Completed just over 61 percent of his passes for 2,043 yards with 22 touchdowns to go against 7 interceptions. Also ran for 83 yards and two more scores in 11 games.2020: Region 5-AAAAAAA Co-Offensive Player of The Year. Guided the Warriors to an 8-3 record and a first-round playoff appearance; Competed at the Georgia Elite Classic.

name: Tanner Bailey
scouting_report: Smaller framed quarterback at just over 6-foot tall, and about 190 pounds. Natural pocket passer with some functional mobility but not a downfield running threat. Immense intelligence passing the football and understanding spacing and timing. Has the right trajectory on the football. Anticipatory and above average accuracy. Very adept throwing the short to intermediate passing game and has nice touch on the outside planes. Easy stroke and release. Doesn't have a cannon but makes up for it with accuracy and ball placement. Makes a high percentage of advanced level seam throws between defenders. Tough player that took his team to a championship as a junior and played banged up.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Played first six games of junior season before suffering a collarbone injury, but returned for final four games of the season; completed 60 percent of his throws and helped Gordo to a 13-2 record and an Alabama 4A state runner-up finish.

name: MJ Morris
scouting_report: A legit 6-foot-1, pushing 6-foot-2. Has tested exceptionally well at different points during his prep career making him a true dual-threat talent. A touch passer. Comfortable throwing on the run, but likes to establish a sturdy base when he can and square up to his target. Some of his best balls can come on quick one and three-step drops. Capable of attacking the deeper parts of the field, but could always find some more velocity and dial in the accuracy. Averaged 6.5 yards per carry as a sophomore. Didn’t have his number called on many designed runs as a junior, but that’s likely due to the fact that he injured his shoulder. Ability to move the chains with his legs and improvise when things break down makes one of the more intriguing quarterback prospects in the class of 2022. Will need to keep developing over the next few years and get more consistent, but has some of the required traits to win games at the Power 5 level. Likely to excel in a spread offense with RPO concepts.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2020: Transferred into Atlanta (Ga.) Pace Academy. Missed some time due to injury.2019: Started 12 games at Carrollton (Ga.) High where he accounted for 2,565 yards of total offense and 18 touchdowns.

name: Joshua Wood
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundJoshua Wood is a 6-3, 185-pound Dual-Threat Quarterback from Graham, WA. His dad is former Washington State standout and 1988 Aloha Bowl MVP Victor Wood.

name: Nicco Marchiol
scouting_report: Prototype build and frame. Quick-release left-hander with ability to slide the pocket. Impressive presence and anticipation. Goes through progressions well and shows terrific rhythm to find targets. Steps up when he has to and keeps composure under duress. Quick release with ability to throw on the run. Athletic enough to pick up yards down field. Upside as multi-year starter, All-Conference type performer and NFL Draft Day 3 selection.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPlayed his freshman and sophomore seasons at Aurora (Colo.) Regis Jesuit before moving to Arizona with his family.2020: Played 8 of 10 games for Chandler (Ariz.) Hamilton as a junior.

Athletic BackgroundTwo-sport athlete who also plays basketball.\Helped San Antonio Southside to a Texas 5A second-round playoff appearance as a sophomore, scoring 21 points in a first-round win.2021: Played three games as a senior before suffering a season-ending knee injury.2020: Texas District 14-5A D-I Offensive MVP as a junior.

name: Conner Harrell
athletic_background: H.S. name: Noah Fifita
scouting_report: Small frame and short in stature. Limited physically without much growth potential. Dynamic playmaker in the nation's toughest high school league. Shows knack for maintaining eyes down field while scrambling. Displays decent arm strength and good accuracy. Repeatedly throwing mechanics and generates easy torque. Flashes ability to necessary loft on passes into tight spaces. Makes good decisions with the football and puts good touch. Looks in rhythm when stepping up in the pocket to fire down field. Shows wiggle in open space to buy extra yards. Doesn't force throws and tucks the ball to fight for positive plays. Despite diminutive height, stays away from big hits and doesn't go down easily. Could could to improve overall speed and add zip on deep routes. Potential starter at Power Five level later in college career.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundNoah Fifita is a Quarterback from Anaheim, CA.

name: Peter Delaportas
scouting_report: Excellent frame with shoulder width to add 15 pounds. High-level athlete and very good wrestler. Great competitor and leader. Very good at pre-snap reads. Spins ball well and throws catchable ball. Nice touch on deep passes. Trusts arm to get into tight windows. Shows ability to extend plays. Accurate on the move. Needs to work through progressions. Has to continue to improve arm strength. Must get stronger in upper body. Sometimes uses a lot of shoulder movement to add zip to throw. Locks onto receivers. Multi-year starter at Top 20 program. Day 3 NFL draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPeter Delaportas is a 6-3, 195-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Sparta, NJ.

name: Adryan Lara
scouting_report: Broad-shouldered frame with strong lower half. Strong-armed quarterback with ability to air it out downfield. Shows nice touch on deep throws. Creates a lot of torque with his upper body and can sling ball from different angles. Displays pocket awareness and ability to make pass-rushers miss. Can extend plays while keeping his eyes down field. Delivers the ball on time and leads receivers into space. Stands in the pocket while facing pressure, but at times could make better decisions. Could also continue to refine drop-back footwork while shortening long wind-up motion. Projects as eventual Power Five starter with chance to garner NFL Draft attention.
